text: .” “Okay, out of unnecessary trouble , then .” She slipped her hand beneath his shirt, fingertips grazing his abdomen. The sensation danced across his sensors. Her eyes and cheeks were reddened from her tears, but he’d never seen her so beautiful as she was in that moment . “What will we do after all this?” she asked . It was not something he’d considered yet; his processing power was so caught up in the countless scenarios for the next day that he hadn’t devoted any thought to the day after. Their success — and their survival — was too uncertain . “I don’t know,” he said, after a long pause . “I want to return to your house .” Ronin tilted his head. “After all this, you still want to go back to Cheyenne ?” She flattened her palm against his stomach. “It was never the place, it was the ruler. Without Warlord, Cheyenne can be rebuilt. Your house…it became home for me. A real home, and it’s where I fell in love with you .” The frantic simulations slowed, his processors shifting toward the present. Toward Lara . Death wasn’t an issue for bots. As he’d once told her, there was on and off . Despite his core programming’s tendency — oft ignored — to err on the side of self-preservation, he did not fear his eventual deactivation. Hell, he’d been through it at least twice, already . But the idea of her death, of a future without Lara…that instilled true fear inside him. Irrational fear, the kind that whispered in distant corners of his consciousness, preying upon his inadequacies. How could he revert to a directionless life after she was gone ? “Then that is where we’ll stay,” he replied. “Though…if you still want to go, I promised I would take you to the ocean .” “Yes. I want to see the endless ocean, like in that book.” Her hand slid higher, sweeping over his chest and brushing his nipples. She shifted her hips, skirt falling to reveal her thighs, and smoothed her foot along his calf. “We can see the ocean, and then go back home. We could even tell the neighbors about it, but most of them probably wouldn’t believe us .” Electric jolts of pleasure pulsed through Ronin as she caressed his skin. Holding himself up on one arm, he placed a hand on her thigh and skimmed it toward her hip, bunching the fabric of her skirt around his wrist. She shivered beneath his touch, releasing a soft, shuddering breath . “It won’t matter what they think,” he said, focusing his optics on her lips . “Not even a little.” She spread her knees, opening herself to him . “You’re all that matters, Lara Brooks .” She smiled. Her hand stopped at the center of his chest, directly over his core processors. “Love me, Ronin .” He eased over her, nestling his hips between her thighs . “Long after the Dust claims me .” Chapter Thirty - Two L ara had spent countless nights huddled beside Tabitha in one drafty shelter or another, desperate for warmth. They suffered through blistering heat and freezing rain, bodies ravaged by hunger, weariness, and pain. Together, they’d survived the worst Cheyenne could throw at them for more years than should have been possible . Those years, those hardships, had not prepared Lara for her first glimpse of her old home after months away . The morning sun had not yet crested the horizon, leaving the sky a dull, dreary gray. The hobbled shacks were dark shapes against that sky, grave markers for the not-yet dead. Even the people who weren’t sick or starving here were dying. She’d never forget the despair and hopelessness that had gripped her during her time in the slums. Only Tabitha had kept Lara from succumbing to it…but she’d been claimed by this place , too . Another body for the heap . Newton and handful of men had broken off to wait on the outskirts of the slums while Lara guided Cooper and his team along the narrow dirt pathways between the shacks. Before she led them to the center of town, to the one place everyone had to go — the water pump — they had a stop to make . The solid metal of the ring brushed over Lara’s skin beneath her shirt, a reminder of what she stood to lose — everything . She and Ronin hadn’t said goodbye. All they needed to say had been communicated the night before, through their words and their bodies . She adjusted her hood and dropped her hand to the pistol on her thigh, focusing on the feel of its grip rather than the stench of rot and human waste. People were just beginning to emerge from their homes. Even in the dim light, Lara noted the recognition in some of their eyes. What did they see? The thin, desperate waif she’d been, or the healthy, well-fed woman she’d become ? Or, did some of them see a walking corpse ? Lara forced her eyes ahead as she neared her destination. She had a clear mission, and it would require her full attention. Gary and Kate were respected and well-liked by many, and they’d always been kind to Lara; if she could convince them to join her, it’d be a damn good start . Her steps faltered as their shack came into view . “No,” she breathed . Warlord’s symbol was on their door, red paint dripping like blood . She rushed across the remaining distance and fumbled to shove the door aside . The foul odor of old blood assaulted her nostrils when she entered. Behind her, the bootsteps of her escort were frantic as they hurried to catch up. A faint light came from the small, secondary room. A man stepped in front of it.
